channel.title: +Kokutai on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +Kokutai on Vishvas's notes

item.title: Selective assimilation of foreign thought
item.description: Central pattern Since the beginning, Japan has constantly been a student-culture, rather than (mostly) inward looking smug know-it-all-culture like medieval India and China (in the post Tang era). Yet, they revered their own nation as the land of the kAmi, and were not swayed by anything and everything. The Chinese, for example, considered whatever happened outside China much less important. This led to the defeats of the Qing empire [T1] at Japanese hands.
